heading: Coopers Shoot vs Cooplacurripa — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Coopers Shoot, New South Wales and Cooplacurripa, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Coopers Shoot has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Cooplacurripa (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Coopers Shoot or Cooplacurripa safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Coopers Shoot has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Cooplacurripa scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Coopers Shoot scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Cooplacurripa scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Coopers Shoot or Cooplacurripa?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Coopers Shoot has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.42% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
